text.title: Hybrid Search Rules and Failure Modes
text.summary: Concrete operational rules, BM25 parameter tuning, production gotchas, and failure modes for hybrid search systems.

rule.statement: Set BM25 parameters for your content: short document chunks need b=0.3-0.5; long documents need b=0.75; technical content needs k1=1.5-2.0; general content k1=1.2. Match BM25 tokenizer to corpus language.
rule.rationale: Default BM25 parameters (k1=1.5, b=0.75) are calibrated for long English prose. Applying them to short chunks or code degrades keyword matching significantly.

rule.statement: Log retriever attribution: which retriever contributed each successful result. After 2 weeks, if BM25 contributes <10% of final results, hybrid may not be justified for that query distribution.
rule.rationale: Hybrid adds complexity and ~20% latency. If your query distribution is purely semantic, the BM25 component is waste. Data-driven decisions about disabling hybrid prevent unnecessary complexity.

rule.statement: Before switching from pure vector to hybrid in production, A/B test on 5% of traffic and measure a quality metric. Latency + complexity cost must be justified by measurable gain.
rule.rationale: Hybrid is not always better. On conversational or creative corpora, BM25 can add noise. Measure on real traffic, not on a benchmark that may not reflect your query distribution.

antipattern.description: Using raw BM25 scores in linear combination without normalization.
antipattern.reason: BM25 scores for a 10K-document corpus can range 0-30+; vector similarity scores range 0-1. Without min-max or z-score normalization, BM25 dominates entirely and linear combination degrades to pure BM25.

antipattern.description: Two parallel network calls for manual fusion without timeout handling.
antipattern.reason: If vector DB is slow but BM25 is fast, waiting for both adds tail latency. Set a timeout on the slower call and fall back to the fast one's results alone.

antipattern.description: Using Qdrant hybrid with a standard BM25 library instead of a sparse encoder (SPLADE).
antipattern.reason: Qdrant hybrid requires sparse vectors (e.g., SPLADE) as the sparse component — not the same as a Python BM25 library. Ensure the sparse encoder is deployed and version-pinned.

antipattern.description: Fetching top-10 for fusion and then reranking the same 10.
antipattern.reason: The reranker can only reorder what it receives. Fetch top-100 for fusion, rerank the pool, return top-10. A relevant document at rank 15 is invisible to a reranker that receives only 10 candidates.
